project.name: llama Mail
project.description: A self-hosted IMAP web client with automatic keyword labeling powered by a local Ollama model. Polls unread mail, classifies messages via LLM, applies IMAP keywords, and provides a full browser UI for reading, config, notifications, and compose.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Binary entry point that delegates to the app package, wiring config migration and the Run orchestrator.

nodes.name: client.go
nodes.summary: Thin adapter that exposes SelectLabelFromText, the interface boundary the poller uses to request a keyword label from an Ollama/llama backend.

nodes.name: http_client.go
nodes.summary: HTTP client for an Ollama/llama server that warms up and pulls models, paces and retries classification calls, and parses allowed-label responses with noise stripping.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a mutex-guarded warmupState singleton plus dedicated append-only log helpers for llama server/output/error streams.

nodes.name: HTTPClient
nodes.summary: Client struct holding base URL, model, and tuning template that drives warmup, model pull, and paced/retried classification against the llama HTTP API.

nodes.name: Classify
nodes.summary: Runs a single message through the llama model to select a keyword label, ensuring warmup, pacing, and one-shot classification with noise handling.

nodes.name: NewHTTPClient
nodes.summary: Constructs an HTTPClient from base URL, API key, model, and tuning template settings.

nodes.name: ParseAllowedLabels
nodes.summary: Parses the model's raw response into the set of allowed labels, filtering tool-only and empty-message noise.

nodes.name: LoadTuningText
nodes.summary: Loads per-user tuning/prompt template text used to bias the classification prompt.

nodes.name: contacts_handlers.go
nodes.summary: HTTP handlers for the CardDAV-backed contacts feature: list/get/create/delete contacts, bulk delete, dedupe, DAV password management, and incremental sync.

nodes.name: handleContactsSync
nodes.summary: Handles incremental contact synchronization, applying client change sets against a base cursor and returning changed records since the cursor.

nodes.name: handleContactByID
nodes.summary: Handles get, update, and delete of a single contact by UID, mapping payloads to and from the contacts store.

nodes.name: handleContactsBulkDelete
nodes.summary: Deletes multiple contacts in one request, aggregating per-UID success and failure results.

nodes.name: handleContactsDAVPassword
nodes.summary: Reads, sets, and clears the hashed CardDAV app password used to authenticate DAV clients against the contacts store.

nodes.name: dav_auth.go
nodes.summary: HTTP Basic Auth middleware for CardDAV endpoints, with a TTL credential cache keyed per user to avoid repeated password verification.

nodes.name: davCredentialCache
nodes.summary: Mutex-guarded cache of resolved DAV auth contexts with expiry and per-user invalidation.

nodes.name: withDAVBasicAuth
nodes.summary: Wraps a handler to enforce HTTP Basic authentication for DAV requests, consulting and populating the credential cache.

nodes.name: mfa_handlers.go
nodes.summary: HTTP handlers for TOTP-based multi-factor auth: status, setup, confirm, disable, and recovery-code regeneration, gated by password confirmation.

nodes.name: handleMFAConfirm
nodes.summary: Confirms MFA enrollment by validating a TOTP code against the pending secret and enabling TOTP with fresh recovery codes.

nodes.name: handleMFASetup
nodes.summary: Begins MFA setup by generating a pending TOTP secret and returning a provisioning URI for authenticator apps.

nodes.name: handleMFAStatus
nodes.summary: Returns the current user's MFA state, including whether TOTP and push MFA are enabled.

nodes.name: push_mfa_handlers.go
nodes.summary: HTTP handlers for push-based MFA approvals: enabling push MFA, enrolling native approver devices, dispatching challenges, and polling/finishing/responding to approvals.

nodes.name: handlePushRespond
nodes.summary: Processes an approver device's approve/deny response to a push MFA challenge, validating device ownership and resolving the challenge state.

nodes.name: handleMFAPushEnabled
nodes.summary: Enables or reports push MFA availability for the user, checking that approver devices are registered.

nodes.name: dispatchPushChallenge
nodes.summary: Sends a push MFA challenge notification to the user's registered approver devices.

nodes.name: server_users.go
nodes.summary: Admin-only HTTP handlers for user management: list, create, update, reset password, activate/deactivate, clear MFA, and last-admin protection.

nodes.name: handleUsersCreate
nodes.summary: Creates a new user account with a parsed role and initial password, enforcing admin authorization.

nodes.name: handleUsersUpdate
nodes.summary: Updates an existing user's role or attributes, guarding against demoting the last active admin.

nodes.name: app.go
nodes.summary: Application composition root that loads config, constructs the user store, llama client, poller, health monitor, and HTTP server, then runs the daemon and server together.

nodes.name: Run
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that loads configuration, wires all dependencies into runDeps, and starts the daemon and server based on the requested mode.

nodes.name: runDeps
nodes.summary: Aggregate of constructed dependencies (config, store, users, health, logger) passed to the daemon and server runners.

nodes.name: runAll
nodes.summary: Runs the poller daemon and HTTP server concurrently, coordinating shutdown between them.

nodes.name: newLlamaClient
nodes.summary: Builds the llama HTTP client from configuration, resolving base URL, model, API key, and tuning template.

nodes.name: migrate.go
nodes.summary: One-time migration that relocates legacy single-user state and config files into the new per-user directory layout.

nodes.name: migrateLegacySingleUserData
nodes.summary: Copies legacy single-user state, IMAP config, and tuning files into the first admin user's directory when the new layout is empty.

nodes.name: config.go
nodes.summary: Configuration model and loader for the backend, covering llama, scan, rate limit, redaction, label, and notification settings plus VAPID/notification key material and per-user settings.
nodes.languageNotes: Generates and persists a VAPID/notification key pair on first init via loadOrCreateNotificationPrivateKey.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Root configuration struct aggregating timezone, log level, llama, scan, rate limit, redaction, label, and notification settings.

nodes.name: LoadOrInit
nodes.summary: Loads configuration from disk or initializes defaults, ensuring notification key material exists before returning.

nodes.name: loadOrCreateNotificationPrivateKey
nodes.summary: Loads the VAPID/notification private key from disk or generates and persists a new key pair for web push signing.

nodes.name: service.go
nodes.summary: Thread-safe health service tracking overall healthiness, AI credit exhaustion, and native push success/failure state for reporting and monitoring.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: Mutex-guarded health tracker exposing setters/getters for health status, AI credit exhaustion, and native push success/failure signals.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Snapshot struct of health state: healthiness, failure reason, AI credit exhaustion, and native push failure details.

nodes.name: logger.go
nodes.summary: Minimal thread-safe logger wrapping the standard library logger with Info/Error levels and a pluggable writer.

nodes.name: Logger
nodes.summary: Mutex-guarded logger with leveled Info/Error methods writing to an underlying writer.

nodes.name: rotate.go
nodes.summary: Size-based rotating file writer that rolls the current log file and prunes old files once a maximum size and file count are reached.

nodes.name: rotatingWriter
nodes.summary: io.Writer that opens, rotates, and closes a log file based on max size and retained-file limits.

nodes.name: native_sender.go
nodes.summary: Native push dispatcher that sends FCM/APNs notifications through a relay Worker, handling relay key resolution, device registration, and stale-response detection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses per-platform prefixes to resolve separate FCM and APNs relay senders from env and key files.

nodes.name: NativePushDispatcher
nodes.summary: Selects the correct FCM or APNs relay sender for a device platform and dispatches native push messages.

nodes.name: RelaySender
nodes.summary: HTTP sender that posts push payloads to a Cloudflare relay Worker using a relay URL and API key.

nodes.name: registerWithRelay
nodes.summary: Registers this backend instance with the push relay Worker so it can send notifications on the shared free tier.

nodes.name: poller.go
nodes.summary: Core background daemon that polls each user's IMAP inbox, classifies unread messages via the llama client, applies keyword labels, and dispatches web/native push notifications with rate limiting and health tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-user aware: maintains per-user stores, mail clients, and mail caches keyed by user ID behind a mutex.

nodes.name: Poller
nodes.summary: Central daemon struct owning config, per-user state, llama client, health, native push dispatcher, and rate limiter that drives the scan/classify/label/notify loop.

nodes.name: tickUser
nodes.summary: Runs one scan cycle for a single user: fetches unread messages, updates the mail cache, and classifies/labels/notifies each new message.

nodes.name: handleMessage
nodes.summary: Classifies a single message with retry, applies the selected keyword label, records the decision, and triggers notifications.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.summary: Top-level scan tick that iterates over all active users and dispatches a per-user tick under a concurrency semaphore.

nodes.name: maybeSendPushNotification
nodes.summary: Decides whether a classified message warrants a web push notification and sends it to the user's subscriptions.

nodes.name: maybeSendNativePushNotification
nodes.summary: Decides whether to send a native (FCM/APNs) push notification for a classified message and dispatches it to registered devices.

nodes.name: push_dispatch.go
nodes.summary: Push delivery helpers that fan out web push to browser subscriptions and native push to registered devices, tracking sent/failed/removed/queued outcomes.

nodes.name: SendWebPush
nodes.summary: Sends a web push notification to all of a user's browser subscriptions, pruning expired ones and returning delivery counts.

nodes.name: SendNativePushToDevices
nodes.summary: Dispatches a native push message to a set of registered devices via the native dispatcher, tracking sent, failed, removed, and queued results.

nodes.name: redact.go
nodes.summary: Regex-based redaction engine that applies configured patterns to scrub sensitive text before it is sent to the classifier or logs.

nodes.name: Engine
nodes.summary: Holds compiled redaction patterns and applies them to input text via Apply.

nodes.name: retry.go
nodes.summary: Small generic retry helper that loops an operation with backoff until it succeeds or attempts are exhausted.

nodes.name: Loop
nodes.summary: Runs an operation repeatedly with delay between attempts until it succeeds or the attempt budget is exhausted.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Per-user persistent state store backing checkpoints, processed-message tracking, classification decisions, web/native push subscriptions, pull notifications, AI credit status, and desktop pairing codes on disk.
nodes.languageNotes: Loads and atomically persists a single stateFile JSON with dirty-flag tracking and mutex-guarded refresh-from-disk helpers.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Mutex-guarded persistent state store exposing checkpoint, decision, subscription, native device, pull notification, AI credit, and desktop pairing operations.

nodes.name: persistLocked
nodes.summary: Serializes the in-memory state into the stateFile struct and atomically writes it to disk.

nodes.name: UpsertNativeDevice
nodes.summary: Inserts or updates a registered native push device, preserving MFA-approver flags and refreshing timestamps.

nodes.name: ConsumeDesktopPairingCode
nodes.summary: Validates and single-use consumes a desktop pairing code, enforcing rate limits and recording the attempt.

nodes.name: totp.go
nodes.summary: Self-contained TOTP implementation generating secrets and provisioning URIs and validating time-based one-time codes with a drift window.

nodes.name: Validate
nodes.summary: Validates a submitted TOTP code against a secret, checking adjacent time steps to tolerate clock drift.

nodes.name: GenerateSecret
nodes.summary: Generates a random base32 TOTP secret for enrolling a new authenticator.

nodes.name: users.go
nodes.summary: User account store handling load/migration, creation, roles, activation, password hashing (scrypt), and TOTP/push MFA and recovery-code state persisted to a users file.
nodes.languageNotes: Passwords and recovery codes are hashed with scrypt; TOTP secrets stored encrypted (TOTPSecretEnc).

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: User record holding credentials, role, activation state, and MFA fields (TOTP secret, recovery code hashes, push MFA flag).

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Mutex-guarded user store persisting accounts to a JSON file and exposing CRUD, role, password, and MFA mutations.

nodes.name: LoadOrMigrate
nodes.summary: Loads the users file or migrates legacy admin-env credentials into an initial admin account, upgrading the on-disk format as needed.

nodes.name: Create
nodes.summary: Creates a new user with hashed password and role, enforcing username uniqueness before persisting.

nodes.name: HashPassword
nodes.summary: Derives a scrypt password hash with a random salt, encoded for storage in the users file.

nodes.name: verifyScryptHash
nodes.summary: Parses a stored scrypt hash and verifies a candidate password against it in constant time.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component that handles session/auth state, top-level tab routing across all admin pages, and shared compose/draft state for the mail client.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large functional component (~870 lines) holding most cross-page state via useState hooks.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Main application component that gates on authentication, renders the tab navigation, and dispatches to the active page while sharing compose-draft state.

nodes.name: readFileAsAttachment
nodes.summary: Reads a browser File into a base64 attachment payload via FileReader for use in the compose flow.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Thin fetch wrapper providing JSON GET/PUT/POST/DELETE helpers with unified error extraction for the backend API.

nodes.name: requestJSON
nodes.summary: Core fetch helper that issues a JSON request, parses the response, and throws a normalized error on non-OK status.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Client-side normalization helpers for server configuration objects, including label de-duplication and keyword-mapping shaping.

nodes.name: normalizeConfig
nodes.summary: Coerces a raw config object from the API into a well-formed, defaulted config shape used by the UI.

nodes.name: normalizeKeywordMappings
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw keyword-to-label mapping input into a consistent structure with unique labels.

nodes.name: contacts.ts
nodes.summary: Contacts/CardDAV API layer exposing CRUD, dedupe, DAV password management, client sync, bulk delete, and import/export operations against the backend.

nodes.name: importContacts
nodes.summary: Uploads a contacts file (vCard/CSV) to the backend import endpoint via multipart form data.

nodes.name: users.ts
nodes.summary: User-administration API layer wrapping listing, creation, role changes, password reset, (de)activation, and MFA clearing endpoints.

nodes.name: auth.tsx
nodes.summary: Defines the React AuthContext and the useAuth hook for accessing the current session across the app.

nodes.name: PageTabs.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable pagination tab-strip component that renders numbered page buttons and reports selection to the parent.

nodes.name: PageTabs
nodes.summary: Renders a horizontal set of page-number buttons with active highlighting and an onSelect callback.

nodes.name: usePagination.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hook that slices an items array into pages and returns current-page data plus page metadata.

nodes.name: usePagination
nodes.summary: Computes total pages and the current page slice for a given items array and page size.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Vite/React bootstrap entry point that applies the stored theme and mounts the App component into the DOM root.

nodes.name: ConfigPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin configuration page for editing labeling keywords, timezone, theme, and CardDAV/server settings with text<->model conversion helpers.

nodes.name: ConfigPage
nodes.summary: Renders and manages the full server-configuration editing form, loading, mutating, and persisting config via the API.

nodes.name: textToMapping
nodes.summary: Parses free-text keyword-mapping input into a structured keyword-to-labels object.

nodes.name: ContactsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Contacts management page with paginated listing, add/edit/delete forms, dedupe, bulk operations, and import/export against the contacts API.

nodes.name: ContactsPage
nodes.summary: Renders the contacts CRUD UI, coordinating listing, editing, dedupe, and bulk actions through the contacts API layer.

nodes.name: formStateToInput
nodes.summary: Converts editable contact form state into the API input payload, diffing against the original contact.

nodes.name: contactToFormState
nodes.summary: Maps a contact record into the editable form state shape used by the contact editor.

nodes.name: HealthPage.tsx
nodes.summary: System health dashboard page that polls the backend health endpoint and renders uptime and service status.

nodes.name: HealthPage
nodes.summary: Fetches and displays backend health/status metrics with periodic refresh.

nodes.name: formatDuration
nodes.summary: Formats a duration in seconds into a human-readable uptime string.

nodes.name: LoginPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Authentication page handling login and setup modes, including TOTP MFA challenge flow, calling the auth API and reporting session changes.

nodes.name: LoginPage
nodes.summary: Renders the login/setup form, submits credentials and MFA codes, and propagates authentication results upward.

nodes.name: LogsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Log viewer page that lists backend log files as tabs and streams/renders the selected file with per-line level highlighting.

nodes.name: LogsPage
nodes.summary: Top-level log page that fetches the list of log files and renders a tabbed LogViewer.

nodes.name: LogViewer
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders the contents of a single log file with level-based line styling and refresh.

nodes.name: NotificationsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Notifications settings page for managing push-notification preferences, keyword triggers, and native device pairing (token/QR links) with the push backend.

nodes.name: NotificationsPage
nodes.summary: Manages notification preferences and device pairing UI, coordinating keyword selection and push-registration state with the backend.

nodes.name: buildNativePairingLink
nodes.summary: Constructs the deep-link URL used to pair a native mobile device for push notifications.

nodes.name: ReadPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Primary mailbox reading page: lists inbox messages, renders sanitized email HTML, and builds reply/reply-all/forward drafts with attachment support.

nodes.name: ReadPage
nodes.summary: Renders the inbox list and message viewer, handling selection, refresh, and reply/forward draft creation.

nodes.name: processEmailHtml
nodes.summary: Sanitizes and rewrites raw email HTML, optionally blocking remote images, for safe rendering in the viewer.

nodes.name: buildForwardBody
nodes.summary: Builds the quoted forward-message body including original headers from a source email.

nodes.name: buildReplyAllRecipients
nodes.summary: Computes the reply-all recipient set from an email's from/to/cc address lists.

nodes.name: SecurityPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Account security page for managing TOTP MFA enrollment, recovery, and related security settings via the backend.

nodes.name: SecurityPage
nodes.summary: Renders and manages the user's security settings, including TOTP setup and verification flows.

nodes.name: TuningPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Admin page for tuning the Ollama labeling model parameters and reviewing labeling behavior.

nodes.name: TuningPage
nodes.summary: Renders the model-tuning controls and persists tuning settings through the API.

nodes.name: UsersPage.tsx
nodes.summary: User administration page listing accounts and providing role changes, password resets, (de)activation, and MFA clearing for admins.

nodes.name: UsersPage
nodes.summary: Renders the user list and admin actions, invoking the users API for management operations.

nodes.name: theme.ts
nodes.summary: Theme registry and runtime theming utilities that apply CSS variables, persist the chosen theme to localStorage, and restore it on load.
nodes.languageNotes: Applies theming by writing CSS custom properties directly to the document root.

nodes.name: getStoredTheme
nodes.summary: Reads the persisted theme name from localStorage, falling back to a valid default when unset or invalid.

nodes.name: applyTheme
nodes.summary: Applies a named theme by writing its CSS variables to the document and persisting the selection.

nodes.name: client.go
nodes.summary: IMAP adapter that connects to a mail server and implements mailbox listing, message/overview fetching, label and folder management, inbox actions, attachment retrieval, and draft/sent saving. Loads encrypted stored credentials via cryptutil.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a Client interface and an APIClient implementation guarded by a mutex with lazy connection establishment.

nodes.name: Client
nodes.summary: Interface describing the mail-client contract: unread listing, overviews, bodies, labels, folder CRUD, inbox actions, attachments, and draft/sent saving.

nodes.name: APIClient
nodes.summary: Concrete IMAP client implementation holding connection state and stored-config credentials, providing all Client interface operations against a live IMAP session.

nodes.name: NewAPIClientFromStoredConfig
nodes.summary: Constructs an APIClient that reads its IMAP credentials from an encrypted stored-config file on demand.

nodes.name: ApplyInboxAction
nodes.summary: Applies an inbox action (archive, delete, mark read/unread, move) to a message by ID, resolving source and target mailboxes and issuing the corresponding IMAP commands.

nodes.name: ListSubfolders
nodes.summary: Lists child mailboxes under a parent path, resolving hierarchy delimiters and filtering to direct subfolders.

nodes.name: carddav_client.go
nodes.summary: Outbound CardDAV client: stores encrypted remote address-book credentials, discovers address books, fetches vCards over HTTP, and syncs remote contacts into the local contacts store.
nodes.languageNotes: Parses DAV multistatus XML responses to enumerate address books and cards.

nodes.name: syncCardDAVClient
nodes.summary: Fetches remote address-book cards and upserts them into the local contacts store, reconciling by remote contact UID.

nodes.name: fetchAddressBookCards
nodes.summary: Issues a CardDAV REPORT/PROPFIND request against an address-book path and parses the multistatus response into remote cards.

nodes.name: handleContactsCardDAVClientSync
nodes.summary: HTTP handler that triggers an on-demand CardDAV client sync and returns the resulting status.

nodes.name: dav_server.go
nodes.summary: Inbound CardDAV server backend exposing the local contacts store as address objects, translating between Contact records and vCard representations for DAV clients.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a CardDAV backend interface with per-user principal and address-book-home-set resolution.

nodes.name: contactsDAVBackend
nodes.summary: CardDAV backend implementation backing address-book and address-object operations with the per-user contacts store.

nodes.name: contactToVCard
nodes.summary: Serializes a Contact record into a vCard string including names, emails, phones, addresses, and revision metadata.

nodes.name: contactFromVCard
nodes.summary: Parses a vCard payload into a Contact record, extracting names, typed values, and addresses.

nodes.name: PutAddressObject
nodes.summary: Handles a CardDAV PUT of an address object, converting the vCard to a Contact and upserting it into the store.

nodes.name: server.go
nodes.summary: Central HTTP API server wiring routes for IMAP config, mail send/draft/attachments, inbox listing and actions, notifications and native/web push, desktop pairing, auth with TOTP/recovery MFA, tuning, logs, and frontend serving.
nodes.languageNotes: Very large Server struct aggregating dozens of net/http handlers, session and auth-context middleware, and pairing-token JWT-style signing.

nodes.name: Server
nodes.summary: Primary API server aggregate holding config, logger, health service, user store, mail/contacts/cache dependencies, sessions, and the full set of HTTP handlers.

nodes.name: NewServer
nodes.summary: Constructs a Server with its config, logger, health service, user store, and config-update callback wired in.

nodes.name: Run
nodes.summary: Registers all HTTP routes and middleware, then starts and serves the API listener.

nodes.name: serveInbox
nodes.summary: Core inbox rendering logic that syncs the mail cache against the live IMAP client, builds tabbed keyword scaffolding, and returns the inbox view with delta support.

nodes.name: handleMailSend
nodes.summary: HTTP handler that decodes a mail request, builds the MIME message, and sends it over SMTP with TLS and timeout handling.

nodes.name: handleLogin
nodes.summary: Authenticates a user by password, starting a session or escalating to an MFA challenge when required.

nodes.name: handleMFATOTP
nodes.summary: Validates a TOTP code against an active MFA challenge and completes login on success.

nodes.name: handleNotificationNativeRegister
nodes.summary: Registers a native push device, normalizing its platform and persisting it in the state store for notification delivery.

nodes.name: handleInboxActions
nodes.summary: HTTP handler that applies inbox actions (archive, delete, move, mark) to selected messages via the IMAP client and updates the cache.

nodes.name: createPairingToken
nodes.summary: Mints a signed, time-limited pairing token bound to a subscriber ID for desktop/native device pairing.

nodes.name: server_userscope.go
nodes.summary: Per-user scoping helpers resolving user-specific config/state directories and lazily building per-user state, contacts, mail-cache, and IMAP client instances plus subscriber-index lookups.

nodes.name: userMailClient
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches a per-user IMAP mail client from that user's stored config paths.

nodes.name: resolveMailAuthContext
nodes.summary: Resolves the authenticated user's mail authorization context for a request, selecting the correct per-user mail client.

nodes.name: lookupUserBySubscriber
nodes.summary: Maps a push subscriber ID back to its owning user by consulting the cached subscriber index.

nodes.name: rescanSubscriberIndex
nodes.summary: Rebuilds the in-memory subscriber-to-user index by scanning each user's notification subscriptions and native devices.

nodes.name: contacts.go
nodes.summary: Core contacts domain model defining the Contact record with typed values and addresses, revision-based ETag computation, and tombstone marking for deletions.

nodes.name: Contact
nodes.summary: Contact record with name fields, typed emails/phones, addresses, revision, and deletion state; computes an ETag from its revision.

nodes.name: dedupe.go
nodes.summary: Contact deduplication engine: normalizes emails/phones/names into match keys, clusters duplicates with union-find, decides mergeable groups, and merges members into a single survivor record.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a union-find structure to cluster contacts sharing any normalized match key.

nodes.name: findDuplicateGroups
nodes.summary: Clusters contacts into duplicate groups by unioning records that share any normalized match key.

nodes.name: mergeGroup
nodes.summary: Merges a group of duplicate contacts into one survivor, unioning values and addresses and tracking absorbed UIDs.

nodes.name: matchKeys
nodes.summary: Derives the set of normalized match keys (emails, phones, name) used to identify duplicate contacts.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Persistent, mutex-guarded contacts store backed by a JSON file, providing list/get/upsert/delete, revision-based change tracking, deduplication, and garbage collection of tombstones.
nodes.languageNotes: Atomic file writes and disk-refresh-on-read keep the in-memory map consistent with the JSON backing file.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Mutex-guarded contacts store holding the in-memory contact map, base directory, and revision counter with JSON persistence.

nodes.name: New
nodes.summary: Creates a contacts Store rooted at a base directory and loads any existing contacts from disk.

nodes.name: Upsert
nodes.summary: Inserts or updates a contact, bumping the revision and persisting the change atomically to disk.

nodes.name: Dedupe
nodes.summary: Runs the deduplication engine over stored contacts, merging duplicate groups and returning a report of merges applied.

nodes.name: GC
nodes.summary: Garbage-collects tombstoned contacts older than the retention window and persists the pruned set.

nodes.name: cryptutil.go
nodes.summary: Symmetric encryption helper providing key load/create, envelope parsing, and seal/open of encrypted payloads used to protect stored credentials and secrets.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps AEAD sealing with a base64 envelope format persisted to a key file.

nodes.name: Seal
nodes.summary: Encrypts a payload under the given key and returns an EncryptedPayload envelope with nonce and ciphertext.

nodes.name: Open
nodes.summary: Decrypts an EncryptedPayload envelope with the given key, returning the original plaintext.

nodes.name: LoadOrCreateKey
nodes.summary: Loads an encryption key from a file or generates and persists a new one if absent.

nodes.name: fsutil.go
nodes.summary: Small filesystem utility providing atomic file writes (temp-file-plus-rename) and RFC 4122 UUIDv4 generation.

nodes.name: AtomicWriteFile
nodes.summary: Writes a file atomically by writing to a temporary file and renaming it into place with the given permissions.

nodes.name: NewUUIDv4
nodes.summary: Generates a random RFC 4122 version-4 UUID string.

nodes.name: mailcache.go
nodes.summary: Mail cache domain model defining cached message entries and overviews with metadata-equality checks and conversion from IMAP overviews, plus sync-result reporting.

nodes.name: Entry
nodes.summary: Cached mail message entry holding overview fields, status, and metadata used for delta comparison during cache sync.

nodes.name: entryFromOverview
nodes.summary: Builds a cache Entry from an IMAP message overview, copying metadata fields.

nodes.name: store.go
nodes.summary: Persistent, mutex-guarded mail cache store keyed by mailbox window, supporting snapshot reads and delta sync against live IMAP data with atomic JSON persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains per-mailbox windows and reconciles cached entries against live overviews to compute deltas.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Mutex-guarded mail cache store holding per-mailbox windows with JSON-file backing.

nodes.name: Sync
nodes.summary: Reconciles the cached mailbox window against live IMAP entries since a checkpoint, computing additions/updates/removals and persisting the result.

nodes.name: Snapshot
nodes.summary: Returns a bounded snapshot of cached entries for a mailbox window up to the given limit.

nodes.name: Upsert
nodes.summary: Inserts or updates cached entries for a mailbox window and persists the updated cache atomically.

nodes.name: mailmsg.go
nodes.summary: MIME message builder that assembles headers, body, and base64-wrapped attachments into an RFC-compliant email message for SMTP delivery.
nodes.languageNotes: Emits multipart MIME with base64 line-wrapping for attachment encoding.

nodes.name: Message
nodes.summary: Email message model holding headers, body, and attachments, able to build a full MIME wire representation.

nodes.name: Build
nodes.summary: Assembles the message into a MIME byte payload, writing headers, body, and base64-encoded multipart attachments.

nodes.name: mfa.go
nodes.summary: In-memory multi-factor-authentication challenge store managing TOTP attempt/step consumption, push-approval resolution, recovery-code generation, and sealing/opening of encrypted TOTP secrets.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines a transient challenge store with cryptutil-backed sealing of persistent TOTP secrets.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: In-memory store of active MFA challenges keyed by challenge ID with TOTP and push-approval lifecycle methods.

nodes.name: Create
nodes.summary: Creates and stores a new MFA challenge for a user, returning its identifier.

nodes.name: ResolvePush
nodes.summary: Resolves a push-based MFA challenge by recording the approving device's approve/deny decision.

nodes.name: GenerateRecoveryCodes
nodes.summary: Generates a set of random single-use recovery codes for account MFA fallback.

nodes.name: SealTOTPSecret
nodes.summary: Encrypts a base32 TOTP secret under the encryption key so it can be stored at rest.

nodes.name: OpenTOTPSecret
nodes.summary: Decrypts a sealed TOTP secret with the encryption key, returning the base32 secret for validation.

nodes.name: base64url.ts
nodes.summary: Shared base64url encoding helpers used by both push-relay workers when constructing JWT provider tokens for FCM and APNs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses browser/Workers-runtime btoa and TextEncoder rather than Node Buffer for base64url conversion.

nodes.name: push-relay-common.ts
nodes.summary: Shared core of the push-relay Cloudflare Workers: API-key minting/hashing, admin auth, rate limiting, self-registration, and KV-backed key lifecycle used identically by the FCM and APNs relays.
nodes.languageNotes: Targets the Cloudflare Workers runtime: KV (env.API_KEYS), rate-limiter bindings, Analytics Engine writeDataPoint, and Web Crypto (crypto.subtle).

nodes.name: apns.ts
nodes.summary: APNs transport layer: imports the ES256 auth key, mints and caches JWT provider tokens, and posts push payloads to Apple's HTTP/2 push endpoint with stale-token retry handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Signs provider JWTs with ECDSA P-256 (ES256) via crypto.subtle and caches tokens in a Workers Cache-like object.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Entry point for the APNs push-relay Worker: authenticates senders, enforces rate limits, and routes /send, /register, and /admin/keys requests, delegating delivery to sendApnsMessage.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports a default Workers fetch handler; wraps routing with per-request correlation logging.

nodes.name: fcm.ts
nodes.summary: FCM transport layer: signs a service-account JWT assertion, exchanges it for an OAuth2 access token (cached), and posts messages to the FCM v1 send endpoint with stale-token retry handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses RS256 (RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 / SHA-256) service-account signing and a token-exchange fetch to obtain Google OAuth2 access tokens.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Entry point for the FCM push-relay Worker: authenticates senders, enforces rate limits, and routes /send, /register, and /admin/keys requests, delegating delivery to sendFcmMessage.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports a default Workers fetch handler; mirrors the APNs worker's routing structure over a shared common module.

nodes.name: base64UrlEncode
nodes.summary: Encodes a byte array to a URL-safe, unpadded base64 string.

nodes.name: base64UrlEncodeString
nodes.summary: Encodes a UTF-8 string to base64url by delegating to base64UrlEncode.

nodes.name: timingSafeEqual
nodes.summary: Constant-time string comparison used to check the admin secret without leaking timing information.

nodes.name: requireAdmin
nodes.summary: Verifies the bearer token against the configured admin secret using a constant-time comparison.

nodes.name: checkMinuteLimit
nodes.summary: Applies the per-key per-minute rate limit via the Workers rate-limiter binding, returning whether the request is allowed.

nodes.name: recordUsageAnalytics
nodes.summary: Writes a per-request usage data point to the Cloudflare Analytics Engine binding for observability.

nodes.name: resolveExpiry
nodes.summary: Normalizes a request-supplied expiry (ISO date or day count) into an ISO expiry timestamp for a minted key.

nodes.name: mintKey
nodes.summary: Generates a new random API key, hashes it, and persists the key record plus its index entry to KV.

nodes.name: handleAdminCreate
nodes.summary: Admin endpoint handler that validates the request body and mints a labeled API key with a resolved expiry.

nodes.name: handleRegister
nodes.summary: Self-registration handler that (when enabled) revokes any prior key for the caller and mints a fresh device-scoped API key.

nodes.name: handleAdminList
nodes.summary: Admin endpoint handler that lists all stored API keys from KV, annotating each with its expiry state.

nodes.name: revokeKeyById
nodes.summary: Deletes an API key record and its associated index entries from KV given the key id.

nodes.name: importApnsPrivateKey
nodes.summary: Parses the APNs PKCS#8 PEM auth key and imports it as an ES256 signing key via Web Crypto.

nodes.name: generateProviderToken
nodes.summary: Builds and ES256-signs an APNs provider authentication JWT (header/claims/signature) from the APNs config.

nodes.name: getProviderToken
nodes.summary: Returns a cached APNs provider token or mints and caches a fresh one when absent or near expiry.

nodes.name: isStaleResponse
nodes.summary: Classifies an APNs error status/reason as a stale-token condition that warrants a token refresh and retry.

nodes.name: sendApnsMessage
nodes.summary: Sends a push payload to Apple's APNs HTTP/2 endpoint, attaching the provider token and retrying once on a stale-token rejection.

nodes.name: handleSend
nodes.summary: Handles POST /send for the APNs worker: authenticates the API key, checks expiry and rate limits, records analytics, and dispatches the message via sendApnsMessage.

nodes.name: route
nodes.summary: Request router for the APNs worker dispatching /send, /register, and /admin/keys endpoints to their shared handlers.

nodes.name: importPrivateKey
nodes.summary: Parses the FCM service-account PKCS#8 PEM key and imports it as an RS256 signing key via Web Crypto.

nodes.name: signServiceAccountAssertion
nodes.summary: Builds and RS256-signs the Google service-account JWT assertion used to request an OAuth2 access token.

nodes.name: getAccessToken
nodes.summary: Returns a cached OAuth2 access token or exchanges a signed assertion at Google's token endpoint and caches the result.

nodes.name: isStaleResponse
nodes.summary: Classifies an FCM error status/reason as an auth-staleness condition that warrants a token refresh and retry.

nodes.name: sendFcmMessage
nodes.summary: Sends a message to the FCM v1 endpoint using a bearer access token, retrying once on a stale-auth rejection.

nodes.name: handleSend
nodes.summary: Handles POST /send for the FCM worker: authenticates the API key, checks expiry and rate limits, records analytics, and dispatches the message via sendFcmMessage.

nodes.name: route
nodes.summary: Request router for the FCM worker dispatching /send, /register, and /admin/keys endpoints to their shared handlers.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build that compiles the Go backend and Vite frontend, then assembles a Node.js runtime image bundling llama-lab, Ollama, and supervisor, exposing port 5866.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-stage build: golang builder for the backend binary, node builder for the frontend dist, and a slim node runtime that installs Ollama and runs under supervisord.

nodes.name: backend-builder
nodes.summary: Golang build stage that downloads modules and compiles the backend into a static /app/bin/llama-lab binary from ./cmd/main.go.

nodes.name: frontend-builder
nodes.summary: Node build stage that installs npm dependencies and produces the production frontend bundle via npm run build.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose file that builds and runs the single llama-lab service, wiring up named volumes for config/private/logs/state plus a host-mounted Ollama models directory and a rich set of environment variables.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ${VAR:-default} interpolation for all env vars and mounts persistent named volumes so container state survives restarts.

nodes.name: llama-lab
nodes.summary: The llama-lab container service: builds from the local Dockerfile, publishes port 5866, sets custom DNS, and persists config, private secrets, logs, state, and Ollama models via volumes.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file documenting all runtime configuration for the llama Mail stack, including the Ollama base URL and model cache, tuning file path, server base URL, mobile pairing secret, and the push/APNs relay endpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Dotenv-style KEY=value pairs covering Ollama, IMAP/SMTP session, mobile pairing, and Cloudflare Worker relay URLs.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Contributor and AI-agent guidance document defining the 'ponytail' lazy-senior-dev coding stance, the DOX documentation framework (read-before-edit, update-after-edit, doc hierarchy), and an ultra-lite AI governance policy.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project documentation for llama Mail covering features, architecture, requirements, quick-start setup, users and roles, environment variables, mobile pairing, Cloudflare push relays, persistence, API highlights, operations, and troubleshooting.
nodes.languageNotes: 22 sections spanning setup, API surface, and troubleshooting for a self-hosted IMAP client with Ollama labeling.

nodes.name: TUNING.md
nodes.summary: Prompt-tuning template for the Ollama email classifier that defines the allowed labels in priority order, classification rules, per-label definitions and triggers, and the expected input/output contract.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Backend module guide documenting the Go email-classification engine's purpose, internal package layout, HTTP API contract, environment variables, key data/log files, and per-user multi-tenant data model for agents working in the backend.
nodes.languageNotes: AGENTS.md convention: a per-directory contract file describing ownership, contracts, and verification steps, with links to child DOX index files.

nodes.name: go.mod
nodes.summary: Go module manifest for llama-lab/backend (Go 1.26.4) declaring direct dependencies including go-imap, webpush-go, emersion/go-webdav (CardDAV), yaml.v3, and golang.org/x/crypto, plus transitive indirect requirements.

nodes.name: go.sum
nodes.summary: Cryptographic checksum lockfile pinning exact versions and hashes of all direct and transitive Go module dependencies for reproducible, verifiable backend builds.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Frontend contributor guide describing the React 18 SPA architecture, the mandatory src/api/client.ts HTTP layer, AuthContext/RBAC route gating, per-page API endpoint mappings, theme system, auth flow, and build/verification commands.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains Page-to-API and App-Shell-to-API mapping tables that act as the source of truth for endpoint usage.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point that mounts the React app into #root via /src/main.tsx and preloads fonts, favicons, theme color, and the PWA web manifest.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend npm manifest defining Vite dev/build/preview scripts and dependencies (React 18, React Router 6, Quill WYSIWYG editor, qrcode) plus TypeScript and Vite tooling.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the frontend enabling strict mode, ES2021/DOM libs, bundler module resolution, react-jsx transform, and noEmit type-checking over src.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Documents the scripts/ directory contract: container init, process orchestration via supervisord, and the startup sequence (entrypoint → bootstrap → services) plus Ollama model management.

nodes.name: bootstrap.sh
nodes.summary: One-shot first-run bootstrap that creates config/log/state directories and, on a fresh install, generates scrypt-hashed admin credentials into admin.env for the backend to import into users.json.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an inline Node.js scrypt (crypto.scryptSync) invocation to hash the seed admin password; idempotent guard skips when users.json or admin.env already exists.

nodes.name: entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Container entrypoint that creates the runtime directories, chowns them to the llamalab user, and execs supervisord to orchestrate all services.

nodes.name: pull-ollama-model.sh
nodes.summary: One-shot script that waits for the Ollama API to become available, verifies the models directory is writable, and pulls the configured OLLAMA_MODEL. Idempotent and safe to re-run.

nodes.name: start-ollama.sh
nodes.summary: Launches the Ollama daemon (ollama serve) on the configured host/port with the models directory prepared, run as a supervised long-running service.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Maintainer and self-hoster guide for the APNs push relay Cloudflare Worker, covering KV/APNs key setup, self-registration, per-server API key auth, endpoints, deployment, and troubleshooting.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents the maintainer-hosted relay pattern: self-hosted Go servers forward Bearer-authenticated push requests to the Worker, which holds the single APNs provider key.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: npm manifest for the APNs push-relay Cloudflare Worker, declaring wrangler dev/deploy/typecheck scripts and TypeScript/wrangler dev dependencies.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the APNs push-relay Worker, scoping compilation to the src directory with Cloudflare Workers types.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the Llama Labels Push Relay Cloudflare Worker, covering one-time maintainer setup, box self-registration, manual key issuance, HTTP endpoints, rate limiting, usage, and observability.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: npm manifest for the push relay Cloudflare Worker, defining the deploy/dev scripts and Wrangler/TypeScript dev dependencies.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the push relay Worker, targeting the src directory with Cloudflare Workers-compatible compiler options.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code project settings defining the tool permission allowlist for this repository.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent-facing contract doc for the adapters package, describing the IMAP and Ollama HTTP client subpackages, ownership, and verification rules.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent-facing contract doc for the contacts package covering purpose, ownership, local contracts, and verification guidance.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent-facing contract doc for the mailcache package describing local contracts, ownership boundaries, and how to verify changes.

nodes.name: Desktop_Pairing.md
nodes.summary: Detailed implementation guide for the desktop notification pairing feature, covering the pairing API endpoint, storage/retrieve/delete flows, and usage examples.

nodes.name: 2026-07-10-apn-relay-deployment-design.md
nodes.summary: Design and deployment spec for the APNs relay Cloudflare Worker, covering architecture, Apple credential setup, wrangler configuration, and validation steps.

nodes.name: 2026-07-12-contact-dedupe-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for server-side contact deduplication and merge, defining the matching detail, merge policy, data model changes, and response shape.

nodes.name: llamalabels.xcf
nodes.summary: GIMP source image (XCF) for the Llama Mail branding artwork used to produce app icons.

nodes.name: manifest.webmanifest
nodes.summary: PWA web app manifest declaring the Llama Mail app name, standalone display mode, theme colors, and icon set.

nodes.name: sw.js
nodes.summary: Service worker handling Web Push: refreshes VAPID subscriptions on pushsubscriptionchange, renders push notifications, and focuses/opens the target URL on notification click.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the Push API and Notification API within service-worker lifecycle events (push, pushsubscriptionchange, notificationclick).

nodes.name: agpl-3.0.txt
nodes.summary: Full text of the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 bundled with the frontend for license display.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the frontend, providing the complete visual design system (layout, theming, components) for the Llama Mail web client.

nodes.name: vite-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript ambient declaration referencing Vite client types for the frontend build.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration enabling the React plugin and setting the dev server port for the frontend.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent-facing contract doc for the share directory outlining its purpose, ownership, and verification guidance.

nodes.name: supervisord.conf
nodes.summary: Supervisord process configuration for the container, orchestrating bootstrap, API server, background daemon, Ollama, and model-pull programs with logging and restart policies.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines multiple [program:*] blocks with priorities to sequence startup of the API, daemon, and Ollama processes inside one container.

nodes.name: wrangler.toml.example
nodes.summary: Template wrangler configuration for the APNs relay Cloudflare Worker, documenting KV namespace bindings and required secrets.

nodes.name: wrangler.toml.example
nodes.summary: Template wrangler configuration for the Web Push relay Cloudflare Worker, documenting KV namespace bindings and required VAPID secrets.

nodes.name: SelectLabelFromText
nodes.summary: Deterministic label picker: scans model output text and returns the first matching allowed label, prioritizing 'Questionable' then substring matches then 'Important'.

layers.name: Frontend UI
layers.description: React/Vite single-page app shell, route pages, shared components, hooks, theming, and PWA assets that render the mail client and its admin screens.

layers.name: Frontend API Client
layers.description: Browser-side fetch wrappers and typed clients that call the Go backend for config, users, and contacts data.

layers.name: Backend HTTP API
layers.description: Go HTTP server, app bootstrap, and REST/CardDAV handlers for auth, MFA, users, contacts, and health that expose backend functionality to the frontend.

layers.name: Mail Processing & Classification
layers.description: IMAP polling, message parsing, and the Ollama/llama adapter that fetches mail and assigns keyword labels via the AI backend.

layers.name: Auth & MFA
layers.description: User account store plus TOTP and multi-factor authentication logic securing access to the mail client.

layers.name: Persistence & State
layers.description: On-disk state, mail cache, contacts storage, and configuration persistence backing the backend services.

layers.name: Push Notifications
layers.description: Backend push dispatch, shared push-relay TypeScript library, and the Cloudflare Worker FCM and APNs relays that deliver web/native notifications.

layers.name: Shared Utilities
layers.description: Cross-cutting Go helpers for crypto, filesystem, logging, log rotation, redaction, and retry used throughout the backend.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Deployment
layers.description: Dockerfiles, docker-compose, supervisord, provisioning shell scripts, and all build/project configuration for the single-container deployment.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: README, AGENTS guides, tuning notes, design specs, and the license text describing the project and its subsystems.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what llama Mail is: a self-hosted IMAP web client that polls unread mail and auto-applies keyword labels using a local Ollama model, all wrapped in a single-container deployment. The backend module guide (backend/AGENTS.md) complements it by laying out the Go package structure, the HTTP API contract, and the per-user multi-tenant data model you will meet throughout this tour. Read these first to build a mental map before touching code.

tour.title: Entry Point & Process Model
tour.description: The binary starts in main.go, which runs a one-time config migration and then hands off to the app package's Run orchestrator. app.go is the composition root: it loads configuration, constructs the user store, llama client, poller, health monitor, and HTTP server, then runs the polling daemon and the API server together in one process (dispatched by --mode). This is the single most important file for seeing how every subsystem is wired together.
tour.languageLesson: In Go, a project's runnable command lives under cmd/ and stays thin, delegating real work to internal/ packages. The internal/ directory is special: Go's compiler forbids importing it from outside the module, enforcing a clean public/private boundary.

tour.title: Configuration Model
tour.description: Building on the bootstrap in Step 2, config.go defines the settings model and loader covering llama, scan, rate-limit, redaction, label, and notification options, plus VAPID and notification key material. LoadOrInit reads or creates the config on disk and generates key material on first run, so nearly every subsystem depends on this file (fan-in of 6). Understanding the config shape here makes the rest of the backend legible.

tour.title: The HTTP API Server
tour.description: server.go is the central API surface (the highest fan-out file in the codebase), wiring routes for IMAP config, mail send/draft/attachments, inbox actions, notifications, desktop pairing, auth with TOTP/recovery MFA, tuning, logs, and serving the frontend. Because llama Mail is multi-tenant, server_userscope.go resolves per-user config/state directories and lazily builds each user's own state, contacts, mail-cache, and IMAP client instances. Together they show how one server safely isolates many users' mailboxes.

tour.title: The Classification Pipeline (Heart of the App)
tour.description: poller.go is the core background daemon and the reason this project exists: for each user it polls the IMAP inbox, redacts sensitive text, classifies unread messages via the llama client, applies IMAP keyword labels, and dispatches notifications with rate limiting and health tracking. The Ollama HTTP client warms up and pulls models, paces and retries classification calls, and parses the model's output against the allowed label set. redact.go scrubs sensitive patterns before any text reaches the model or the logs. This is the classification flow the README describes, in code.
tour.languageLesson: The llama package splits a narrow interface (client.go's SelectLabelFromText) from its concrete HTTP implementation (http_client.go). This adapter pattern lets the poller depend on an abstraction, keeping the AI backend swappable and testable.

tour.title: Prompt Tuning Contract
tour.description: TUNING.md is the prompt-tuning template that defines the allowed labels in priority order, the classification rules, per-label triggers, and the exact input/output contract the model must follow. The HTTP client from Step 5 loads this tuning text and validates model output against these labels, so this document is effectively executable configuration. Reading it clarifies why the classifier behaves the way it does and how an operator steers labeling without touching code.

tour.title: IMAP Integration & Mail Cache
tour.description: The IMAP adapter connects to a user's mail server and implements mailbox listing, message and overview fetching, label and folder management, inbox actions, attachment retrieval, and draft/sent saving, loading encrypted stored credentials via cryptutil. The mail cache store persists a mutex-guarded snapshot keyed by mailbox window and delta-syncs against live IMAP data, so the UI and poller avoid re-fetching everything each tick. Outbound mail is assembled by mailmsg.go into a MIME message for SMTP delivery.

tour.title: Persistence, State & Encryption
tour.description: state/store.go is the per-user persistent store backing checkpoints, processed-message tracking, classification decisions, push subscriptions, AI-credit status, and desktop pairing codes on disk (fan-in of 7). cryptutil.go provides the symmetric seal/open used to protect stored IMAP and CardDAV credentials, while fsutil.go supplies the atomic temp-file-plus-rename writes that every store relies on for crash safety (the highest fan-in utility). This layer is how a stateless-looking daemon remembers what it already did.
tour.languageLesson: Atomic writes via write-to-temp-then-rename exploit the OS guarantee that rename() is atomic on the same filesystem, so a reader never sees a half-written file even if the process crashes mid-write.

tour.title: Auth & Multi-Factor Authentication
tour.description: users.go is the account store handling load/migration, roles, activation, scrypt password hashing, and TOTP/push MFA state. totp.go is a self-contained time-based one-time-password implementation, and mfa.go manages the in-memory challenge store for TOTP attempts, push approvals, and recovery codes. The MFA handlers expose setup, confirm, disable, and recovery-code endpoints, gated behind password confirmation, tying the crypto primitives to the HTTP API from Step 4.
tour.languageLesson: TOTP (RFC 6238) derives a short-lived code by HMAC-ing the current time step against a shared secret. The drift window lets validation accept the adjacent time steps, tolerating small clock skew between server and authenticator app.

tour.title: Push Notifications: Backend Dispatch
tour.description: push_dispatch.go fans out web push to browser subscriptions and native push to registered devices, tracking sent/failed/removed outcomes, while native_sender.go delivers FCM/APNs notifications through a relay Worker, handling relay-key resolution, device registration, and stale-response detection. The same native dispatch path is reused for push-based MFA approvals in push_mfa_handlers.go, which enrolls approver devices and polls challenge responses. This is the backend half of the notification story that began in the poller.

tour.title: Push Notifications: Cloudflare Worker Relays
tour.description: Because a self-hosted box cannot hold Apple/Google push credentials safely, delivery is proxied through two Cloudflare Workers. The FCM and APNs relay entry points authenticate senders, enforce rate limits, and route /send, /register, and /admin/keys, delegating actual delivery to the FCM and APNs transport layers that mint and cache signed provider JWTs. push-relay-common.ts is the shared core both Workers use for API-key minting, admin auth, rate limiting, and box self-registration. This subsystem is what native_sender.go from Step 10 talks to.
tour.languageLesson: Cloudflare Workers run on the V8 isolate model, not Node.js: there is no filesystem and state lives in bindings like KV. Provider tokens (FCM OAuth2, APNs ES256 JWT) are cached in-memory per isolate and refreshed on staleness to avoid re-signing on every request.

tour.title: Frontend Single-Page App
tour.description: main.tsx applies the stored theme and mounts App.tsx, the root React component that owns session/auth state and top-level tab routing across every admin page plus shared compose/draft state. auth.tsx defines the AuthContext and useAuth hook that gate routes by role, and client.ts is the mandatory thin fetch wrapper every page uses to call the Go backend. ReadPage.tsx is the primary mailbox view, rendering sanitized email HTML and building reply/forward drafts, closing the loop with the mail APIs from Step 4.
tour.languageLesson: React Context (auth.tsx) provides a value to an entire component subtree without prop-drilling; the useAuth hook consumes it. Routing the whole app through a single client.ts fetch wrapper centralizes error handling and auth headers in one place.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Deployment
tour.description: The Dockerfile is a multi-stage build: a Go stage compiles the static llama-lab binary, a Node stage builds the Vite frontend, and a final runtime image bundles the binary, Ollama, and supervisor. supervisord.conf orchestrates the container's processes: bootstrap, the API server, the polling daemon, ollama serve, and the one-shot model pull, matching the process model from Step 2. docker-compose.yml ties it together with named volumes for config, secrets, logs, state, and Ollama models. This is how everything you have seen ships as one container.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use several FROM stages so heavy build toolchains (Go compiler, npm) never reach the final image, keeping it small. supervisord is the classic answer to running multiple long-lived processes in one container, since a container starts only a single PID 1.
